title: LoginData
description:
`login <workspace-address>` — the SESSION mint + the acceptance disclosure (what connecting
delivers). A session = user × workspace × installation, carrying ONE workspace-scoped bearer
credential; further workspaces are further logins. Login IS the acceptance: from here delivery
is silent, npm-style. **INFERRED** (additive-only).

currency.description: The active adapter's auto-update-trigger outcome, when the login armed it.

delivered.description:
How many skills the person's profile delivers here right now (the acceptance disclosure;
best-effort — absent when the count could not be read).

name.description: The workspace's ADDRESS name (the slug typed at `login`).

pending.description: Present while the login awaits the browser approval (re-run `topos login` to resume).

server.description: The API base this installation dials (from the protocol card).

session_id.description: The minted session's id (`sn_…`) — the handle the web sessions pages show.

session_status.description:
`"active"`, `"pending"` while the workspace's session-approval knob holds it (no data flows
until an owner approves), or `"awaiting-approval"` while the browser approval is pending.

triggers.description: The breadth arming sweep's outcomes (one row per other detected agent).

workspace_id.description: The logged-into workspace's id (empty while the login is still pending approval).

BreadthTriggerReport.description:
One breadth-sweep trigger outcome for a DETECTED registry agent (beyond the active adapter's
own [`crate::TriggerReport`]). `agent` is the registry slug; the state/kind pair follows the
same honesty rule everywhere: only `active` carries a live kind, everything else advertises
the explicit-pull floor. **Additive.**

note.description: The consent step still owed, or the evidence-level caveat — `None` when nothing needs saying.

touched_path.description: The config file the (un)install edited, when it edited one.

CurrencyKind.description:
What fires the update check for a harness — drives HONEST receipt copy ("current by next session",
"within about a minute", …). `ExplicitPullOnly` is the honest-degrade floor.

oneOf.description:
A session-boundary hook runs the sweep (Claude Code's SessionStart; Hermes's
`on_session_start`/`on_session_reset`) — updates are current when a session begins.

A harness-scheduled recurring job runs the sweep (OpenClaw's silent cron) — updates land
within the cadence while the harness's scheduler is running.
No automatic trigger is provably live — updates land on an explicit `topos update`.

EnrollmentPending.description:
A pending device-authorization a `follow` surfaced — the human opens `verification_uri` and
enters `user_code` there (or the invitation page the URI names weaves them through); the client
re-polls. The code never rides a URL. **INFERRED.**

expires_at.description: The session expiry as an RFC-3339 string, if it expires.

interval_secs.description: The minimum poll interval, in seconds — a headless agent re-invokes no faster than this.

user_code.description:
The short human-facing code the approval page asks for and displays back (the glance-check
against this terminal — never typed as a secret, never part of a URL).

verification_uri.description:
The page the human opens to approve the session — the bare approval address (or, for an
invitation enrollment, the invitation page that weaves accept + approval). Never embeds the
code.

TriggerReport.description:
The result of installing a auto-update trigger — what was touched + the marker, so re-install is
idempotent (the marker is a managed sentinel block in the config edit, NEVER in skill bytes).

marker_id.description: `topos` + harness id + schema version + command identity.

touched_path.description: The config file edited (never a skill dir).

oneOf.description: An equivalent command exists without our marker — adopt-or-leave, never blind-append.
